text:
The tiny turtle found itself on a beautiful day gliding. It was lazy, soaking up the sunlight. It floated elegantly down a little brook, enjoying the cool water.

Suddenly, it heard a tiny voice.

"Can't swim?"

The turtle turned its head to silence. Of course, it could swim. It was just enjoying a lazy float. But then it did what it usually did to look around — it lowered its head slightly. That's when it noticed the girl standing beside the brook.

She looked tiny against the riverbank, her blond hair bouncing. But she was earnest.

"I didn't mean to scare you," she said. "Is it even fair to chase fish with your nose? That's such funny business."

The turtle blinked slowly. Nothing about this felt like play. Maybe it was just curious.

"Fair," it agreed finally. "Here?"

"I've followed you here." She pointed. "You've been chasing this bird for hours."

The turtle looked
